title: dipterocarpus stellatus subsp. parvus
intro: Dipterocarpus stellatus subsp. parvus is a subspecies of the Dipterocarpus stellatus tree, commonly found in Southeast Asia, known for its unique star-shaped leaves. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties, which are derived from its bark and leaves. The health benefits of Dipterocarpus stellatus subsp. parvus include its ability to treat fever, malaria, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels. Traditional herbal preparations involve boiling the bark and leaves in water to create a tea or infusion, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
